    Correction Factors

    Automatic cubic capacity, cargo-gear and capacity limitation correction factor calculation with required data inputs
    1 fc — Cubic capacity correction factor
    Applicability selection
    Chemical tankersSection 2.2.12.1 fc 1.0000
    Gas carriers (LNG/LPG)Section 2.2.12.2 fcLNG 1.0000
    Ro-ro passenger shipsSection 2.2.12.3 fcRoPax 1.0000
    Bulk carriers (light cargo)Section 2.2.12.4 fc 1.0000
    Ro-ro cargo ships (vehicle carrier)Section 2.2.7 fcVEHICLE 1.0000
    fc — Chemical tankers
    regulation ref: section 2.2.12.1 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fc
    fc(Cubic capacity correction for chemical tankers based on DWT-to-volume ratio.)
    fc result
    1.0000
    fc — Gas carriers (LNG / LPG)
    regulation ref: section 2.2.12.2 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fcLNG
    fcLNG(Cubic capacity correction for gas carriers based on DWT-to-volume ratio.)
    fcLNG result
    1.0000
    fc — Ro-ro passenger ships
    regulation ref: section 2.2.12.3 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fcRoPax
    fcRoPax(Capacity correction for ro-ro passenger ships using DWT / GT ratio.)
    fcRoPax result
    1.0000
    fc — Bulk carriers designed for light cargoes (R < 0.55)
    regulation ref: section 2.2.12.4 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fc (bulk, light)
    fc(Capacity correction for bulk carriers carrying light cargoes where R = DWT / Cargo hold vol < 0.55.)
    fc bulk result
    1.0000
    fcVEHICLE — Ro-ro cargo ships (vehicle carrier) (DWT/GT < 0.35)
    regulation ref: section 2.2.7 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fcVEHICLE
    fcVEHICLE(Cubic capacity correction for ro-ro cargo ships (vehicle carrier) where DWT/GT < 0.35.)
    fcVEHICLE result
    1.0000
    2 fl — Factor for general cargo ships equipped with cranes and cargo-related gear
    Formula / applicability
    fl = fcranes × fsideloader × froro
    fcranes = 1 + Σ(0.0519 × SWL × Reach + 32.11) / Capacity
    fsideloader = Capacity without side loaders / Capacity with side loaders
    froro = Capacity without ro-ro ramps / Capacity with ro-ro ramps
    fl — Factor for general cargo ships equipped with cranes and cargo-related gear
    regulation ref: section 2.2.14 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fl (general cargo, gear)
    fcranes 1.0000
    fsideloader 1.0000
    froro 1.0000
    fl result
    1.0000
    fcranes
    1.0000
    fsideloader
    1.0000
    froro
    1.0000
    3 fi — Capacity factor for technical / regulatory limitation on capacity
    Formula / applicability
    Ice-classed shipSection 2.2.11.1 fi,ice 1.0000
    Voluntary structural enhancementSection 2.2.11.2 fiVSE 1.0000
    CSR bulk carrier / oil tankerSection 2.2.11.3 fiCSR 1.0000
    1.0000
    Ice-classed ship
    regulation ref: section 2.2.11.1 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fi = fi,ice × fiCb
    fi,ice(Combined ice-class and block-coefficient capacity limitation factor.)
    fi result
    1.0000
    Ship-specific voluntary structural enhancement
    regulation ref: section 2.2.11.2 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fiVSE
    fiVSE(Capacity factor for ships with voluntary structural DWT reduction above reference design.)
    fiVSE result
    1.0000
    CSR bulk carrier / oil tanker
    regulation ref: section 2.2.11.3 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fiCSR
    fiCSR(Capacity limitation factor for CSR bulk carriers and oil tankers based on LWT and DWT values.)
    fiCSR result
    1.0000
    4 fm — Factor for ice-classed ships having IA Super and IA
    fm — Factor for ice-classed ships having IA Super and IA
    fmFor IA Super / IA ice-classed ships, fm = 1.0500; all other ice classes = 1.0000.
    ⚠ fm is mandatory for IA / IA Super ice-classed ships and cannot be deselected. To change this, update the Ice Class in Section A.
    MANUAL FM
    1.0000
    5 fj — Ship-specific design elements
    Formula / Applicability
    fj — Ice-classed ships Section 2.2.8.1 · MEPC.364(79) fj 1.0000
    fj — Shuttle tankers Section 2.2.8.2 · MEPC.364(79) fj 1.0000
    fjRoRo — Ro-ro ships Section 2.2.8.3 · MEPC.364(79) & Section 2.2.6 · MEPC.350(78) fjRoRo 1.0000
    fj — General cargo Section 2.2.8.4 · MEPC.364(79) fj 1.0000
    fj — Power correction factor for ice-classed ships
    regulation ref: section 2.2.8.1 of MEPC.364(79) · notation: fj = max(fj0, fj,min) ≤ 1.0
    fj(Power correction factor for ice-classed ships; depends on ship type, ice class, DWT and installed MCR.)
    fj0

    fj(Fixed correction factor for shuttle tankers 80,000–160,000 DWT with dual-engine twin-propeller propulsion redundancy.)

    Required EEXI Setup

    Reference line and reduction factor
    Auto mode includes the required EEXI reduction-factor table by ship type and size, with linear interpolation where the table specifies ranges. Reference-line constants are intentionally editable to align with your workbook or class-approved values.

    EEXI Calculator — Page Guide

    Complete Function & Column Reference

    This calculator computes the Energy Efficiency Existing Ship Index (EEXI) for a vessel, per Resolution MEPC.350(78) (attained EEXI formula and Vref determination) and MEPC.364(79) (Required EEXI reference lines and correction factors fj, fi, fc). Work through Sections A–E in order, click ▶ Calculate, then review the attained vs. required outcome and compliance outlook in Section F.

    Step-by-step workflow

    1

    Enter Ship Particulars (Section A)

    Fill in General Information (shipowner, shipbuilder, hull no., IMO no., Ship Type, Ice class) and Principal Particulars (LOA, LBP, breadth, depth, summer draught, Deadweight). Ship Type and Deadweight drive the reference-line constants, capacity basis, and correction-factor eligibility used everywhere else in the tool.

    2

    Enter Main & Auxiliary Engine data (Section B)

    Enter MCRME, RPM, SFC and fuel type for the main engine(s), and the same for the auxiliary engine(s). If an Engine or Shaft Power Limitation (EPL) is fitted, switch Overridable power limitation to Yes and enter MCRME,lim. If a shaft generator (PTO) is fitted, select it and enter its MCR so it can be deducted from PME.

    3

    Determine the Reference Speed Vref (Section C)

    Choose the applicable Vref determination case (Case 1 EEDI ship, Case 2 estimated speed-power curve, Case 3 tank test + sea trial, Case 4 statistical approximation) and click the small pencil icon to open that case's source-selection modal — manual entry, the Speed–Power Curve Digitizer, or an available speed-power value table.

    4

    Configure Correction Factors (Section D)

    Tick Apply on any correction factor card that applies to your vessel (fc, fl, fi, fm, fj). Any factor left un-applied defaults to 1.0000 (no correction). fw (weather factor) is fixed at 1.000 in this tool.

    6

    Click "▶ Calculate" and review Results (Section E)

    Press ▶ Calculate in the top toolbar. Section F populates the detailed Attained/Required EEXI worksheets, the compliance chart, the Compliance Indicator bar, the Indicative EPL/Power Target, the KPI cards (Attained EEXI, Required EEXI, Compliance Margin, Status) and the results table.

    Top toolbar buttons

    Button
    What it does
    ▶ Calculate
    Runs every EEXI calculation using the current Section A–E inputs and populates Section F (worksheets, chart, KPI cards, compliance indicator, results table). Re-click after any change to refresh the results.
    Load Sample
    Populates every field with a representative example vessel so you can see the tool fully working end-to-end before entering your own data.
    Export JSON
    Downloads all current input values (ship particulars, engines, Vref case data, correction factors, required EEXI setup) as a structured JSON file — use it to archive a session or share it with a colleague.
    Import
    Opens a file picker to load a previously exported JSON file. Every field — including fuel rows, Vref case selection and correction-factor settings — is restored exactly as it was when exported.
    Print / PDF
    Generates a formatted A4 compliance report containing all inputs and the attained/required EEXI results. Use your browser's Print → Save as PDF. Run Calculate first so the report includes results.
    Reset
    Clears every input field back to blank/default and hides Section F results. Use this to start a completely fresh calculation.
    ? Help
    Opens this page guide — a complete reference of every workflow step, toolbar button, and input/output column on this page.
    The two circular icons at the far top-left of the page (◀ and ⌂) are simple navigation shortcuts: Go Back returns to the previous page in your browser history, and Go Home returns to the site's home page. Neither one affects your entered data.

    Section A — Ship Particulars

    General information and principal particulars. Free-text identity fields only appear on the printed report; Ship Type, Ice class and Deadweight actively drive the calculation.
    Field
    Meaning
    Shipowner / Shipbuilder / Hull No. / IMO No.
    Free-text vessel identity details. Appear on the printed PDF report only — they do not affect any calculation.
    Ship Type
    Selects the vessel's MEPC.364(79) ship-type category. Determines the reference-line constants (a, b exponent basis, c), the reduction-factor table used in Section E, and which specialty correction factors in Section D are potentially applicable (e.g. gas carrier, bulk carrier, ro-ro).
    Ice class
    N/A, IA Super, IA, IB or IC. Feeds the fm (ice-classed ships) and the ice-classed branch of fj in Section D.
    Length overall / Length BP / Breadth / Depth / Summer draught (m)
    Principal hull dimensions. Used for reference and stored on the report; certain specialty correction-factor formulas (e.g. ro-ro fjRoRo, cranes/side-loader/ro-ro ramp cards) draw on breadth and draught.
    Deadweight (t)
    The vessel's summer deadweight — the primary capacity value used as the denominator throughout the attained EEXI calculation (transport work term), in the Required EEXI reference-line formula, and as the DWT input to every cubic-capacity / capacity-based correction factor.

    Section B — Main Engine & Auxiliary Engine

    Engine particulars feeding the attained EEXI numerator (CO₂ emissions terms).
    Field
    Meaning
    Manufacturer / Type / Number of sets
    Descriptive engine identity and how many identical units are installed. Number of sets multiplies MCR when computing total installed power.
    Overridable power limitation
    Yes/No — whether an Engine Power Limitation (EPL) or Shaft Power Limitation (SPL) is fitted. Selecting Yes reveals Power limitation type and MCRME,lim, and switches the PME basis from 0.75×MCR to 0.83×MCRME,lim.
    MCRME (kW) / RPM at MCR
    Rated Maximum Continuous Rating of the main engine and its rated speed. MCRME is the base value used for the PME power basis (0.75×MCR by default).
    Power limitation type / MCRME,lim (kW)
    Only shown when power limitation = Yes. Choose Shaft or Engine power limitation, and enter the limited MCR value — this becomes the new power basis (0.83×MCRME,lim) for PME.
    SFC input method
    How the Specific Fuel Consumption value is sourced: Manual entry, calculated from NOx Technical File values, or a fixed SFCapp value. For the Main Engine, when "Calculate using NOx Technical File values" is used with Overridable power limitation = Yes, the target load is (PME [kW] / MCRME [kW]) × 100 (%), not a fixed 83%; otherwise the target is 75% MCR.
    SFC at 75% MCRME / 50% MCRAE
    Specific fuel consumption (g/kWh) at the relevant load point, used together with CF to compute the CO₂ emission terms.
    Fuel type / CF
    Select the fuel consumed; CF (tonnes CO₂ per tonne fuel) auto-populates from the MEPC.364(79) carbon-factor table for that fuel and is read-only.
    Shaft generator / MCR PTO unit (kW)
    If a Power Take-Off (PTO) shaft generator is fitted ("Option 1 as per 2.2.5.2"), enter its MCR so the tool deducts the appropriate share from PME per MEPC.364(79) 2.2.5.2.
    PME in kW (summary card)
    Auto-calculated main-engine power basis: MCRME × number of sets × 0.75 (or × 0.83 if power-limited), less any PTO deduction. This value feeds the CO₂ Term 1 in Section F and the Vref digitizer's PME interpolation.
    PAE in kW (Auto / Manual toggle)
    Auto calculates auxiliary-engine power per MEPC.364(79) 2.2.5.6 from total installed main-engine MCR. Manual lets you type a fixed PAE value instead, overriding the automatic formula.

    Section C — Reference Speed Vref

    Determines the vessel's reference speed, used in the transport-work (denominator) term of attained EEXI. Four determination "cases" are available, each mapped to a regulation sub-clause of section 2.2.3 of Res.MEPC.350(78).
    Field / Case
    Meaning
    Vref determination case
    Choose Case 1 (EEDI ship), Case 2 (estimated speed-power curve), Case 3 (tank test + sea trial, EEDI/design load draught) or Case 4 (statistical approximation method). This selects which input block below becomes active.
    Pencil / source-selection icon
    Opens a dedicated modal for the selected case offering three source options: manual entry of a known/verified Vref value, the Speed–Power Curve Digitizer (upload a curve image, calibrate axes, trace the curve, and auto-interpolate Vref at PME), or an available speed-power value table (manual table entry / Excel-CSV import with interpolation).
    Reference speed Vref (display)
    Read-only. The confirmed reference speed (knots) resulting from the selected case and source — this is the value actually used in the attained EEXI denominator.
    Regulation reference
    Records which sub-clause of section 2.2.3 of Res.MEPC.350(78) (2.2.3.1–2.2.3.7) supports the chosen Vref determination method, for the printed report.
    Case 3 fields — VS,EEDI, PS,EEDI, PME used
    Sea-trial service speed and the power recorded at that speed, plus the PME from Section B. Vref = VS,EEDI × (PME / PS,EEDI)1/3.
    Case 4 fields — VS,service, DWTS,service, PS,service, k coefficient
    Speed, deadweight and power at the design load draught / service condition. The k coefficient (auto, from ship type/size) is used together with these values in the Case 4 Vref formula.
    Appendix Parameters modal (statistical approximation)
    Displays the looked-up Appendix A–F values, the resulting Vref,avg, MCRavg/MPPavg and mV, and applies the formula Vref,app = (Vref,avg − mV) × (ΣPME / (0.75 × MCRavg))1/3.
    Whichever source you use, always click Confirm Vref Source (or Apply in the Appendix modal) inside that case's modal — this is what writes the final value into Reference speed Vref and refreshes the calculation.

    Section D — Correction Factors

    Five ship-condition correction factors plus a fixed weather factor. Each specialty card has its own Apply checkbox — leave it unticked and the factor defaults to 1.0000 (no correction is applied).
    Factor
    What it corrects for
    fc — Cubic capacity correction
    Applies to chemical tankers (§2.2.12.1), gas carriers LNG/LPG as fcLNG (§2.2.12.2), ro-ro passenger ships as fcRoPax (§2.2.12.3), light-cargo bulk carriers (§2.2.12.4), and ro-ro cargo ships (vehicle carrier) as fcVEHICLE (§2.2.7). Each card takes DWT/Capacity (auto from Section A) and Cargo tank capacity (m³) or GT and computes the Ratio R = DWT/Volume (or DWT/GT) and the resulting fc.
    fl — Cargo-gear factor
    For general cargo ships fitted with cranes, side loaders and/or ro-ro ramps. Selecting "Yes" for cranes / side loaders / ro-ro opens a details popup to enter their lifting/loading capacities, which feeds the combined fl result.
    fi — Technical/regulatory capacity limitation
    Covers ice-classed ships as fi,ice (§2.2.11.1), voluntary structural enhancement as fiVSE (§2.2.11.2), and CSR bulk carriers/oil tankers as fiCSR (§2.2.11.3).
    fm — Ice-classed ship factor
    Applies to ships with IA Super or IA ice class, per the relevant MEPC.364(79) provision.
    fj — Ship-specific design elements
    Four sub-types (click each status chip to switch): ice-classed ships (§2.2.8.1, fj = max(fj0, fj,min) ≤ 1.0), shuttle tankers (§2.2.8.2, fixed fj = 0.77 when 80,000–160,000 DWT with confirmed propulsion redundancy), ro-ro ships as fjRoRo (§2.2.8.3 & §2.2.6 of MEPC.350(78), based on Froude number/breadth/draught/displaced volume), and general cargo ships (§2.2.8.4).
    fw — Weather (sea condition) correction factor
    Fixed at 1.000 in this tool version (read-only) and shown for completeness in the attained EEXI worksheet in Section F.

    Section E — Required EEXI Setup

    Defines the Required EEXI reference line against which the attained EEXI is judged.
    Field
    Meaning
    Required EEXI mode
    Auto uses the built-in reduction-factor (Y%) table by ship type/size (with linear interpolation across DWT ranges) plus editable reference-line constants. Fully manual lets you type the Required EEXI value directly (e.g. from a class-approved workbook), bypassing the auto table.
    Reference line constant a / exponent c
    The MEPC.364(79) reference-line parameters for the vessel's ship type. Editable so you can align them with your workbook or class-approved values if different from the tool's default.
    Reduction factor Y (%)
    Read-only. The phase-in required reduction percentage looked up from the ship type/size table for the vessel's Deadweight.
    Reference line value
    Read-only. Computed as a × Capacity−c using the constants above and the vessel's Deadweight.
    Threshold commentary
    Read-only short note explaining which table row / interpolation was used to derive the reduction factor.
    Required EEXI result
    Read-only. Required EEXI = (1 − Y/100) × Reference line value — this is the figure the attained EEXI must not exceed.

    Section E — Results & Compliance Outlook

    Populated only after clicking ▶ Calculate. Combines a detailed calculation worksheet, an interactive chart, and headline compliance indicators.
    Output
    Meaning
    Attained EEXI calculation worksheet
    Row-by-row build-up: Ship type, fj, Term 1 (main-engine CO₂, gr/hr), Term 2 (auxiliary-engine CO₂, gr/hr), Numerator (total CO₂ emissions), fi, fc, fl, Capacity, fw, reference speed Vref, fm, Denominator (transport work, t·nm/hr), and the final Attained EEXI (g CO₂/t·nm) = numerator ÷ denominator.
    Required EEXI calculation worksheet
    Shows a, b, c, the Required reduction factor X (%), the Reference line value, the final Required EEXI, the percentage reduction of attained EEXI still needed to comply, and an overall Result status.
    Required EEXI Line and Attained EEXI (chart)
    Interactive chart comparing the Required EEXI reference line against your vessel's Attained EEXI point across capacity — hover any point for exact values.
    Compliance Indicator
    A progress bar showing where the attained/required ratio sits: green ≤ 1.000 is compliant, red above 1.000 exceeds the required limit.
    Indicative EPL / Power Target
    An estimate of the maximum compliant PME and the equivalent MCR (or MCRlim) target, based on the currently selected PME mode — useful for sizing an Engine Power Limitation.
    KPI cards — Attained EEXI / Required EEXI / Compliance Margin / Status
    Headline numbers in g CO₂/t·nm, plus the Compliance Margin (positive = compliant headroom, negative = non-compliant) and a Status pill (Compliant / Exceeds / Ready).
    Results table (Check / Value / Comment)
    A line-by-line breakdown of every check performed, its numeric value, and a plain-language comment — useful as a quick audit trail alongside the worksheet.
    EEXI Maritime Calculator · Complete Page Guide · All calculations follow MEPC.350(78) / MEPC.364(79)
    Case 1 — EEDI Ship: Vref Source Selection
    Select the source for Vref. Confirm will update the Case 1 Vref field and refresh the calculation.
    Speed-Power Curve Digitizer — EEDI Speed-Power Curve
    Upload a speed-power curve image from your EEDI file into the digitizer tool. After extracting points, you can apply a correction factor (e.g. BHP→kW conversion), view the corrected curve, and the tool will interpolate Vref at the PME value from Section B automatically.
    Enter available speed-power table values for reference. Use the reviewed / interpolated speed value as the confirmed Vref above.
    Available Speed-Power Value Table
    Open professional manual table / Excel import / Vref interpolation popup. Speed and power values are shown with two decimals.
    Point Speed (kn) Power (kW) Action
    Case 2 — Estimated Speed-Power Curve: Vref Source Selection
    Select the source for Vref. Confirm will update the Case 2 Vref field and refresh the calculation.
    Speed-Power Curve Digitizer — Estimated Speed-Power Curve
    Upload an estimated speed-power curve image into the digitizer tool. After extracting points, apply corrections if needed, and the tool will interpolate Vref at the PME value from Section B automatically.
    Enter available estimated speed-power table values for reference. Use the reviewed / interpolated speed value as the confirmed Vref above.
    Estimated Speed-Power Value Table
    Open professional manual table / Excel import / Vref interpolation popup. Speed and power values are shown with two decimals.
    Point Speed (kn) Power (kW) Action
    ▦
    Estimated Speed–Power Value Table (Case 2)
    MANUAL TABLE · EXCEL IMPORT · VREF INTERPOLATION
    1Power Correction Factor
    Note: If imported power values are in BHP or shaft power, enter the conversion factor. Corrected power = imported power ÷ factor.
    2PME from Section B
    PME (kW) — from engine particulars
    —
    From Section B PME calculation.
    3Calculated Vref
    Confirmed Vref for Case 2
    —
    Import or enter speed-power data to calculate Vref.

    4 Speed–Power Data Table (Case 2)

    # Speed (kn) Power (kW) Power kW after correction Action

    Note: If imported power values are in BHP or shaft power, enter the conversion factor. Corrected power = imported power ÷ factor.

    From Section B PME calculation.

    Import or enter speed-power data to calculate Vref.

    4 Speed–Power Data Table

    # Speed (kn) Power (kW) Power kW after correction Action
    No speed–power data imported yet.
    Speed–Power Curve with Vref Determination
    Blue line = speed-power curve · Orange dashed = PME · Purple dashed = Vref
    Case 3 — Tank Test + Sea Trial (EEDI/Design Load Draught): Vref Source Selection
    Select the source for Vref. Confirm will update the Case 3 Vref field and refresh the calculation.
    Speed-Power Curve Digitizer — Tank Test + Sea Trial (EEDI/Design Load Draught)
    Upload a speed-power curve image from the tank test / sea trial report into the digitizer tool. After extracting points, apply corrections if needed, and the tool will interpolate Vref at the PME value from Section B automatically.
    Enter available speed-power table values from the tank test / sea trial report. Use the reviewed / interpolated speed value as the confirmed Vref above.
    Tank Test + Sea Trial Speed-Power Value Table (EEDI/Design Load Draught)
    Open professional manual table / Excel import / Vref interpolation popup. Speed and power values are shown with two decimals.
    Point Speed (kn) Power (kW) Action
    In case the curve at design load draft (service) is not available and only one service point is available (PS,service, VS,service), Vref at EEXI draft is calculated using the formula below.
    Vref  =  k1/3  ×  (DWTS,service / Capacity)2/3  ×  VS,service  ×  (PME / PS,service)1/3
    Per MEPC.350(78) para 2.2.3.4, no limit applies to the DWT relation when this single-service-point formula is used. The service draft must be on an even keel — a trimmed draft cannot be applied with this formula.
    Calculated Vref (Case 3, single service point formula) —
    ▦
    Tank Test + Sea Trial Speed–Power Value Table (Case 3)

    Note: If imported power values are in BHP or shaft power, enter the conversion factor. Corrected power = imported power ÷ factor.

    Speed–Power Curve with Vref Determination (Case 3)
    Blue line = speed-power curve · Orange dashed = PME · Purple dashed = Vref
    SFC Input Method
    Choose how the SFC value should be determined.
    OPTION 1
    Manual entry
    Directly type in the SFC value (g/kWh) yourself.
    OPTION 2
    Calculate using NOx Technical File values
    Enter the MCR/Load [%] vs SFC [g/kWh] points from the NOx Technical File; the value at the required load is interpolated automatically.
    OPTION 3
    SFCapp fixed value
    Apply the standard SFCapp fixed reference value and fuel type automatically.
    Calculate SFC from NOx Technical File values
    Enter MCR [%] and SFC [g/kWh]. Linear interpolation will be applied.
    Target: 75% MCR
    MCR [%] SFC [g/kWh] Action
    Linear interpolation is used between the nearest lower and higher MCR/load points. If the target is outside the entered range, the nearest available SFC value is used.
    🏗️
    Crane Details for fcranes
    Crane schedule SWL = safe working load · Reach = max outreach
    Crane SWL (t) Reach (m) Action
    ⚠ Please enter valid SWL and Reach values for at least one crane before applying.

    6. Calculated value of attained EEXI

    6.1. Basic data

    Type of ship Capacity / DWT Speed Vref (knots)
    — — —

    6.2. Main engine

    MCRME (kW) PME (kW) Type of fuel CFME SFCME (g/kWh)
    — — — — —

    6.3. Auxiliary engines

    PAE (kW) Type of fuel CFAE SFCAE (g/kWh)
    — — — —

    6.4. EEXI correction factors used

    Correction factor (specific design elements), fj —
    Capacity factor (technical/regulatory limitations), fi —
    Cubic capacity correction factor, fc —
    General cargo / cargo gear correction factor, fl —
    Factor for ice-classed ships, fm —
    Weather / sea condition correction factor, fw —

    6.7. Calculated value of attained EEXI

    Main engine(s) – Term 1
    CFME × PME × SFCME × fj
    = — gCO₂/hour
    Auxiliary engine(s) – Term 2
    PAE × CF,AE × SFCAE
    = — gCO₂/hour
    Correction factors and transport work – Term 5
    fi · fc · fl · Capacity · fw · Vref · fm
    = — t·nm/hour

    6.7.2. Evaluated EEXI

    EEXI = (Term1 + Term2 + Term3 + Term4) / Term5
    —
    attained EEXI: — gCO₂/ton.nm

    6.8. Required EEXI

    a —
    b (Capacity) —
    c —
    Y (required reduction factor) —
    required EEXI: — gCO₂/ton.nm

    Case 4 — Tank test + sea trial: Vref Source Selection
    Select the source for Vref under design load draught. The final value updates Section C and refreshes the EEXI calculation.
    Curve selection
    After digitizing a curve, use Import Speed–Power Data and then Confirm Vref. The imported curve will be stored under the selected curve type. You can repeat this for all required curve types.
